title: agoseris grandiflora
intro: Agoseris grandiflora, also known as tall windflower or great tarweed, is a perennial plant species in the daisy family (Asteraceae) that is native to the western coast of North America. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which have been found to help with wound healing, reducing swelling, and alleviating respiratory issues. The health benefits of Agoseris grandiflora include its ability to soothe digestive issues, reduce fever, and support the immune system. Herbal preparations of the plant include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be made by infusing the leaves and flowers in hot water or a solvent like ethanol, and are often used to treat a range of ailments.
